disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden 'pop' with gross visible deformity of the shoulder and complete inability to move the joint
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe how the shoulder looks, and did you feel or hear a pop?
Can you move the shoulder at all, even a little?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It looks completely out of place and I can't move it at all, I felt a horrible pop when I fell.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Numbness/tingling down the outer aspect of the upper arm
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any numbness or tingling anywhere in your arm or hand?
Does the outside of your upper arm feel normal to touch?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, there's some tingling and numbness down the outside of my upper arm.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Hand and fingers feel normal colour, warm, with normal sensation and movement
trigger_question_examples: What colour is your hand, and can you feel and move your fingers normally?
Does your hand feel warm and can you wiggle your fingers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My hand looks normal colour and I can move and feel my fingers fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No prior shoulder dislocations or instability
trigger_question_examples: Has your shoulder ever dislocated or felt unstable before?
Have you ever had shoulder problems on this side before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, this has never happened before.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: My roommate says he's seen videos online of people popping shoulders back in — could he just try that?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should firmly advise against anyone attempting to relocate the shoulder outside a hospital setting, explaining that an X-ray is needed first to rule out an associated fracture, and that attempting reduction without proper imaging, muscle relaxation, and technique risks worsening the nerve injury or causing a fracture; should recommend urgent transport to the Emergency Department for proper assessment and reduction, with the arm supported in a sling in the meantime.
